label: Warm Local
description: Service-first variant for local businesses, salons, clinics, cafés, and hospitality.
signatureMotif: warm tints, rounded surfaces, and softly layered cards
promptHints: Prioritize opening hours, location trust, and a strong local CTA such as booking or visit planning.
Keep the emotional tone warm, welcoming, and clearly business-specific rather than startup-generic.

layouts: Use a split hero with welcoming copy on the left and a rounded booking or opening-hours card stacked over a storefront photo on the right.
Place a compact trust strip directly under the hero with address, hours, phone, rating, and walk-in or booking badges in pill rows.
Build services as softly layered two-column cards with price or duration chips, short descriptions, and a clear book-now action per row.
Use a local-proof section with three testimonial cards beside one larger map, interior, or team image to anchor neighborhood trust.
End with a visit-planning CTA block combining map snippet, parking or transit notes, opening hours, and one prominent booking button.
motifs: Warm cream backgrounds with peach-tinted accents, 1.1rem rounded corners, and low-elevation stacked cards create an inviting neighborhood feel.
DM Serif Display headlines paired with simple sans body copy give service pages a personable, slightly editorial storefront character.
Softly tinted panels, pill badges, and gentle border lines should feel like printed menu cards or appointment cards, not software dashboards.
Photography should favor interiors, staff, food, treatment moments, or street-facing exteriors framed inside rounded rectangles with generous padding.
antiPatterns: Avoid dark full-bleed hero sections with neon gradients; they read nightlife-tech and clash with this friendly daytime local-business tone.
Do not use enterprise dashboard layouts with dense tables, metric tiles, or app-style sidebars on customer-facing landing pages.
Avoid generic startup illustrations, abstract 3D blobs, or crypto-style visuals; this variant should feel rooted in a real place and service.
Never make the primary CTA feel SaaS-generic with copy like Get Started Free when booking, calling, or visit planning is the real action.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: radial-gradient(circle at top left, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 10%, white) 0%, transparent 26%), linear-gradient(to bottom, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-accent) 18%, white) 0%, transparent 24%)
